mcqs.question: What is the primary operation in Selection Sort?

options.text: Finding the minimum element in the unsorted portion
Inserting elements into their correct position

mcqs.explanation: Selection Sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted portion and swapping it with the first unsorted element.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort in the best case?
mcqs.explanation: Selection Sort has a time complexity of O(n²) in all cases (best, average, and worst) because it always performs n(n-1)/2 comparisons regardless of input order.

mcqs.question: How many swaps does Selection Sort perform in the worst case?
mcqs.explanation: Selection Sort performs exactly n-1 swaps in all cases, which is one of its key properties. This makes it useful when write operations are expensive.

mcqs.question: Is Selection Sort a stable sorting algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Selection Sort is not stable by default because it may change the relative order of equal elements during swaps. However, it can be made stable with modifications.

mcqs.question: When would Selection Sort be a good choice?

options.text: When the array is nearly sorted
When you need a stable sort
When you need O(n log n) performance

mcqs.explanation: Selection Sort is particularly useful when memory writes are expensive because it minimizes the number of swaps (only n-1 swaps).
